title: Dropout Mechanics
concept: Dropout randomly disables a fraction of neurons during training so the network cannot rely on one fixed set of partners every time.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is co-adaptation a problem?

options.text: Because neurons become too dependent on specific partners and may fail when the pattern changes.
Because all neurons must always learn exactly the same feature.
Because the network stops using any weights at all.

exercises.explain: Right. Co-adaptation makes the network brittle because features rely on exact teamwork instead of general patterns.

cards.title: Practice with random teammates missing
cards.body: Imagine a coach who randomly benches different players during practice. Nobody can rely on the same exact teammates every drill, so everyone has to learn how to contribute more independently. Dropout uses this idea inside a neural network.

cards.title: What dropout does
cards.body: Dropout randomly turns off a chosen fraction of neuron activations during training. A neuron that is dropped for that step contributes nothing to the forward pass. On the next step, a different random set may be turned off.

cards.title: Study group with surprise absences
cards.body: If the same two students always explain everything, the rest of the group may coast. But if different people are absent each session, everyone has to understand the material for themselves. Dropout pushes neurons toward that kind of independence.

cards.title: A layer with some neurons muted
cards.body: Suppose a hidden layer has 100 neurons and dropout is set to 30%. During one training step, about 30 of those neurons are randomly muted and 70 stay active. On the next batch, a new random pattern is chosen, so the layer keeps practicing under changing conditions.

cards.title: Dropout does not permanently delete neurons
cards.body: The word “dropout” can sound dramatic, but nothing is thrown away forever. The same neuron might be turned off for one training step and active again on the next. It is a temporary training rule, not a permanent surgery on the model.

cards.title: No dropout vs. dropout during training
cards.body: The key change is whether the layer sees the exact same team every step.

left.text:
All neurons stay active every step.
Same partnerships repeat.
Co-adaptation is easier.

right.text:
Some neurons are randomly turned off.
Teamwork changes each step.
Independent features are rewarded.

options.text: A random fraction of neuron activations is temporarily set to zero for that step.
The optimizer permanently removes the weakest neurons from the network.
The loss function stops being computed.
Every hidden layer is replaced with the output layer.

exercises.explain: Correct. Dropout is a temporary random mute button used during training.

exercises.prompt: True or false: With dropout, the exact same neurons are guaranteed to be turned off on every training step.
exercises.explain: False. The pattern is random, so different neurons may be dropped on different steps.

exercises.sentence: Dropout helps by randomly ___ some neurons during training.
exercises.explain: Yes. Dropout temporarily disables some neurons so the network practices without fixed partnerships.

exercises.prompt: Which setup sounds most like dropout?

options.text: During each batch, a random subset of hidden neurons is muted so the model cannot depend on the exact same pathway every time.
Every training batch re-labels the dataset with new classes.
The network copies one neuron a thousand times to make it stronger.

exercises.explain: Exactly. The defining move is random temporary muting during training.

exercises.prompt: A music-recommendation model uses dropout with a 40% rate in one hidden layer during training. What is the best description of what happens on a typical training step?

options.text: Roughly 40% of that layer's neurons are randomly turned off for that step, and a different random set may be off next time.
Exactly the same 40% of neurons are erased from the model forever.
The output layer is skipped, so the model trains without predictions.
The model duplicates 40% more neurons to compensate for future errors.

exercises.explain: Right. Dropout is random and temporary, not a permanent deletion. Next, we need one more rule: when should dropout be on, and when should it be off?
